updates.tweet_text:
There's 7604 tagged. Another massive bear trap in #ES_F. The squeeze trigger was same as always: A Failed Breakdown. Failed Breakdown of 7527 was the long trigger, we got it at 7:40AM. Targets given 8am were 7588, 7565, 7575, 7587, 7604.

7620 next, then multi-day trend starts https://t.co/K1NlzVmdkO
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7527, 7588, 7565, 7575, 7587, 7604, 7620) are morning-plan levels or hindsight references to targets already given; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Another big bear trap day as we swept the shelf at 7527, recovered, then rallied. Now just ride runner for 7620, 7630, then 7652+. 7587 is nearest support now. Newsletter out soon with next entries
updates.notes: 7652 named as a new upside target ('7652+') not in morning plan; 7620/7630/7587 are morning-plan levels (reaffirmations).

updates.tweet_text: @AnthonyPetril15 Stops on runners go below the prior days low OR beneath a major and clear higher low. Put it under by a few points
updates.notes: Trade management advice about stop placement rules; no specific actionable price level named.

updates.tweet_text: @Shepdog69138782 Eventually, but we are still fairly early in this bull market technically speaking. Topping process itself takes about a year and maybe a couple years before that even starts
updates.notes: Pure macro commentary on bull market timeline; no price level named, no actionable trigger or watch point mentioned.

updates.tweet_text: @EGeorgiu22322 Valid as soon as newsletter comes out
updates.notes: Reply tweet with no price mentioned; purely conversational, no actionable level named.

updates.tweet_text: @NYJets2017 @Shepdog69138782 Markets generally don't care about economics unless there is a major recession/financial crisis, only care about flows, liquidity, and the fact institutions cannot underperform. It all appears on the chart.
updates.notes: Pure market commentary/philosophy tweet; no prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @NYJets2017 @Shepdog69138782 Many people missed the entire covid bull market because they were focused on something that doesn't matter (the economy)
updates.notes: Reply tweet commenting on market psychology/covid bull run; no actionable price levels named whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text:
Yesterday was simple in #ES_F as we work a new range. 7527 Failed Breakdown was long trigger given. We triggered 7:40AM. Targets were 7587 main (hit). Bonus were 7604 (hit), 7620. 7527-7620=the new range

Plan: 7549 (held), 7527 (watch traps)=supports. 7577, 7587, 7604 targets. https://t.co/K1NlzVmdkO
updates.notes: 7549 named explicitly as support ('held') in plan context — not in morning plan. 7527/7577/7587/7604 are all existing morning-plan levels.

updates.tweet_text: @FJN1961 The 7549 setup from the newsletter triggered and played out last evening already. I didn't take that though as It wasn't a high quality Failed Breakdown. I made clear in the newsletter where that setup was. If we don't get that I will just ride runner from 27 yesterday all day
updates.notes: 7549 is a newsletter reference (hindsight/already-triggered), 27 is trade management (runner from prior entry). No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @anchovy_paste Not really because then I would need to predict, and I don't predict as my hardest rule. I'd need a way to know in advance where on that curve an FBD would go (is it average, above, below). That's completely impossible, so it has to be systematized.
updates.notes: Tweet is a conversational reply about methodology/philosophy with no price levels named or actionable signals given.

updates.tweet_text: @anchovy_paste You don't want your human opinions involved anywhere in the trade management process in general. The second you are trading an opinion I can almost guarantee there won't be an edge over a large sample
updates.notes: Reply tweet offering trading philosophy advice; no prices named, no actionable levels referenced whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text:
Low volatility again to start in #ES_F as we remain midrange. 7527 to 7620=the range. Yesterday, the trap of 7527 got us long for the last entry, and we ran to 7587, 7604+ targets.

7549 now support (watch traps), 7527 below which remains key (sell below). 7558, 7577+ upsides https://t.co/EIt29yw0OE
updates.notes: 7549 named as 'now support (watch traps)' and 7577 named as upside target with '+'; neither is in the morning plan.

updates.tweet_text: @tradivate Morphing as all ranges do! Structure actually looking a little more bearish now, fingers crossed
updates.notes: No price levels named at all; purely conversational commentary about market structure and personal sentiment.

updates.tweet_text: @Trade4fun4you @FJN1961 My stops always go under the prior days low which is 7525 now. At the end of today I'll drag it under today's low. My opinions don't factor into my trade management at all its fully automated via my process
updates.notes: Trade management tweet only — describing stop placement process under prior day low (7525 is in morning plan). No new actionable level named.

updates.tweet_text:
Finally getting some volatility expansion in #ES_F (elevator down) which means we can start stalking trades again. 7527-7620 has been the range. Yesterday we trapped 27, got long, rallied to 7604+. Now sub 27 again

7527 must reclaim to rally to 7538, 7549. 7502, 7488, 7473 below https://t.co/180gyHhXWs
updates.notes: 7538 and 7549 named as rally targets if 7527 reclaims — not in morning plan. 7527/7502/7473 are morning plan reaffirmations.

updates.tweet_text: @NerdRageDan Not much volatility but at least its an "elevator down" sell which means I can open up the platform finally!
updates.notes: Pure commentary on market volatility and trade management; no price levels named or actionable triggers given.

updates.tweet_text: @Pete66668332642 @NerdRageDan It depends on the volatility regime but in a strong bull market like we are in now, something like 20-70 points in half an hour or less would be a small elevator down. Social media is also useful here, you will typically see panic/exagerated remarks etc
updates.notes: Pure educational/conversational reply about volatility regimes and social media signals. No actionable price named whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text: @CoryLuc08769351 That is always the entry for a Failed Breakdown as I go over in newsletter everyday. Entry would be when price holds above 7532 for a few minutes.
updates.notes: 7532 is a procedural FBD entry instruction referencing an existing level (~7527/7528 from morning plan), not a newly named actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @franek_to There hasn't been a reclaim of 27 yet, at least not via my rules.
updates.notes: Reply tweet referencing 7527 (morning plan level) with no actionable naming language; purely observational about reclaim status.

updates.tweet_text:
Decent volatility & levels respecting well - this market remains all about 7527. It was the trap here yesterday that got us long to 7604+. Today, we flushed it, and have spent the last 30mins backtesting.

7527 must reclaim to rally today to 7538, 7549+. 7502, 7488, 7473 below https://t.co/XS1DdBSRfe
updates.notes: 7538 and 7549 named as explicit rally targets if 7527 reclaims — not in morning plan. 7502/7473 are morning plan levels; 7488 checked against plan and not listed, but used only as directional reference without naming language.

updates.tweet_text: @experimntal @franek_to I don't use candle closes at all in my system since they are arbitrary. A 5min candle can close above 7533 for example without price actually having spent any time above the level. You want to see price actually holding above the level for a few minutes and ideally pushing
updates.notes: Reply explaining methodology; 7533 is a casual illustrative example, not explicitly named as actionable with naming language.

updates.tweet_text: @Ole_Gregor Exactly! Price will likely be above 7532 by the time that few minutes actually elapses (probably higher 7530s) but thats fine, just means its strong
updates.notes: Conversational reply referencing 7532/7530s as price observation, no actionable naming language; 7527/7528 already in morning plan.

updates.tweet_text: @RobyArnoldi Usually its fairly straight forward, today we have lots of acceptance but the entry still doesn't change. I always err on the side of waiting longer though as newsletter readers know. I have a huge section on how "If you rush, you lose" with Failed Breakdowns
updates.notes: No prices mentioned at all; tweet is methodology/educational commentary about patience with Failed Breakdowns, not an actionable level.

updates.tweet_text: @StephenJ223624 The last few months its been 100% NAP but its just because we are in such a strong bull market and its easiest. In downtrends and when we have deeper failed breakdowns I use the others more
updates.notes: Reply tweet discussing trading strategy/market conditions; no price levels named or actionable entries/exits mentioned.

updates.tweet_text: @NorthoverDaniel @StephenJ223624 non-acceptance protocol, its how I enter most Failed Breakdowns. Most are very fast though (quick sweep of the low then back up). Today we are spending ltos of time accepting below 27 but I'd still take the same entry if we can get it. We'll see if bulls can do it!
updates.notes: Conversational reply explaining entry methodology near 7527 (morning plan level). No new actionable price named; '27' is a reaffirmation of existing 7527.

updates.tweet_text: @aread_22 Can happen anytime in this case! Or not at all. Its better for bulls to see more downside at as always
updates.notes: Conversational reply with no price named; purely directional commentary about bulls preferring more downside — no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text:
Volatility drying now as we enter the mid day chop in #ES_F. Levels continue to respect and its all about 7527. It was support yesterday, trapped, rallied to 7604+ target. Failed today & now resistance for the last hr.

Must recover to rally to 7538, 7549+. 7502, 7488, 7473 below https://t.co/fMPJcIbHJq

updates.tweet_text: @NickYale1 I didn't include that entry in the newsletter for an important reason, because we wouldve lost the 27 shelf.  After a strucutural breakdown the first several failed breakdowns are often risky.
updates.notes: Reply to user explaining trade rationale; no actionable price level named, only a casual reference to '27 shelf' (7527, already in morning plan).

updates.tweet_text:

Downsides were 7502 (hit), 7487 (hit), 7474 (watch traps) https://t.co/yT049fxxoP

updates.tweet_text:
Dip continues in #ES_F and today was all about 7527. Support yesterday, failed today to trigger downside. It then became resistance this morning. Sell targets were 7502 (hit), 7487 (hit), 7474 (hit).

7474 must reclaim to pop. 7458, 7439 below now. 7527 must recover to bottom https://t.co/LysiUcQJQ1

updates.tweet_text: @NerdRageDan Its a dip, not a very big one but cant expect much a parabolic market like this. At least its half a red day though!
updates.notes: Conversational reply about market conditions; no price levels named or actionable instructions given.

updates.tweet_text: @MattLaTurnKnow I dont do anything until we get a planned failed breakdown entry triggering. If its a Mode 1 red day, sometimes this will occur later in the afternoon
updates.notes: Reply explaining trade methodology/timing with no price levels named or actionable triggers identified.

updates.tweet_text: @NerdRageDan Just a normal red day - people have to stop overreacting to normal market moves. I do get it for newer traders especially if just started in the past few months. Anyone trading more than a couple years though panicking at 1.5% dip after weeks of nonstop upside has no excuse
updates.notes: Pure market commentary/reply; no prices mentioned, no actionable levels named. No extraction warranted.

updates.tweet_text: @Trade1233288310 @NerdRageDan Just had a 2.7% pullback under less than a month ago. For a move of this magnitude though that is very shallow still, we have not even had a 5-10% dip yet. Most uptrends you will see at least one 5% dip a month then a 10% dip every few months
updates.notes: Pure market commentary about pullback percentages; no actionable price named, no naming language, no level explicitly identified.

updates.tweet_text: @Trade1233288310 @NerdRageDan In the current parabolic context its unusual but its the context thats not normal not the 2% dip. In normal ES conditions like from last September to March we would get 2% dips often once every two weeks, sometimes more
updates.notes: Pure conversational/contextual commentary about historical volatility patterns. No price levels named, no actionable language used.
